newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6. Context: stock printed an all-time high of $487.90 on June 2 (+5.3% that day) as the AI data-center story gained traction; hedge fund holders rose from 32 to 43 in Q1, Zacks Strong Buy with high momentum score, and GuruFocus flags ~77% overvaluation vs its fair-value model. Today's -4.07% to $445.44 (now ~8.7% off the June 2 ATH) has no identifiable fundamental driver and looks like flow-driven profit-taking after a +69.7% YTD run. Underlying fundamentals unchanged: Q1 sales +19% with industrial +45% on grid/data-center demand (Basler Electric acquisition contributed 39pts of that growth), Q2 guide $690-710M.

sources.label: Insider Monkey / Yahoo Finance - Why Are Investors Turning Bullish on Littelfuse (LFUS)?
GuruFocus - LFUS Up 5.3% but GF Value Says Overvalued (GF Score 82/100)
Littelfuse Shares Rise as AI Data-Center Story Hits
MacroTrends - LFUS price history (ATH $487.90 on Jun 2, 2026)

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at rank 20: today's -4.1% (-8.7% from the June 2 ATH) modestly restores asymmetry on a crowding-penalized list, but with +69.7% YTD, hedge fund count up 32->43 and a Strong Buy consensus, the name remains a rerated, increasingly owned benchmark rather than hidden residual alpha.
verdicts.bull: Pullback offers a slightly better entry into a hard-to-substitute power-protection franchise whose data-center exposure is real, not narrative: industrial sales +45% in Q1 with grid/data-center demand and Basler now consolidated.
verdicts.neutral: A -4% flow-driven dip after a 70% YTD run neither confirms nor breaks anything; the alpha question is residual upside vs ownership, and both moved only marginally today.
verdicts.bear: Crowding is still building (43 hedge funds, momentum screens, bullish retail coverage) while GuruFocus-style fair-value models flag ~77% overvaluation; if AI-passives sentiment cools, a rerated mid-cap gives back multiple, not just momentum.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 print (guide $690-710M) showing data-center/grid demand outrunning guidance, plus Basler cross-sell evidence.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Multiple compression on a crowded, fully rerated name if AI-infrastructure flows rotate; thesis intact but entry alpha already consumed.

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold after yesterday's move up: the node itself is validated (Q1 industrial +45% on data-center/grid), but the 'latent' premise is eroding as the AI data-center story is now widely told - ATH on June 2, rising hedge fund ownership, Strong Buy consensus - so no further promotion without new evidence.
verdicts.bull: Circuit protection and power conversion content per rack rises with data-center power density, and Basler Electric extends LFUS into grid/utility/data-center power systems - the network-centrality case is strengthening, not weakening.
verdicts.neutral: Fundamentals unchanged since June 6; today's -4% is noise against the structural thesis, and rank 20 already reflects yesterday's reconciliation move.
verdicts.bear: Once a latent node is recognized (it just made an all-time high on the AI story), the list's edge - owning it before the market reframes it - is largely spent; what remains is execution risk in a still-cyclical industrial mix.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Continued data-center/grid-driven industrial growth in Q2 confirming LFUS is re-rated as power infrastructure rather than cyclical electronics.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Recognition is already here - latency premium spent - while auto/industrial cyclical segments could disappoint and reassert the old framing at a now-premium multiple.

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at rank 42: no new fundamental information since June 6 and a -4.1% flow-driven pullback does not change the balance the audit already struck between real power-delivery/protection content and a fully recognized +69.7% YTD rerating at $11.25B cap.
verdicts.bull: Q1 showed the power-delivery content thesis working (sales +19%, industrial +45% on data-center and grid demand) with Basler integration adding high-power capability for mission-critical infrastructure.
verdicts.neutral: Yesterday's audit-based hold remains correct; today's dip is profit-taking off an all-time high, not a thesis event in either direction.
verdicts.bear: Valuation models flag substantial overvaluation and a large share of recent growth is acquired (Basler = 39pts of industrial growth), so organic AI-power content may be smaller than the headline numbers imply.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 results against the $690-710M guide and evidence of organic (ex-Basler) data-center protection growth.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Organic growth disappointing once Basler anniversaries, exposing a premium multiple on a partly cyclical revenue base.
